# This is the list of all currently known cycles whose members are greater or equal than to 21 (primitive cycles are omitted).
# All cycles below 10^18 are included in the list.
# 1st line in each block contains the cycle's length and the date of discovery.
# Other lines contain a member of the cycle and its prime factorization.
# There exist 9 non-primitive cycles of length 1, 3 cycles of length 5, 1 of length 7 and 2 of length 8.
# It is unknown why there exist so many cycles of length 5 and none of length 2,3 and 4.

1 2020
65 => 5*13

1 2020
121 => 11*11

8 2020
6699 => 5*13*37*29
69745 => 5*19*7*3*13
25935 => 2*5*7*23*5
8050 => 7*7*31*37
56203 => 5*5*11*7*31
59675 => 5*7*23*37*5
148925 => 13*17*19*2*5
41990 => 3*11*7*29

1 2020
65975 => 5*13*29*7*5

1 2020
701664509 => 7*13*31*53*13*19*19

8 2020
